chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.S-705 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For order on office objection. For hearing of case. 19.12.2025 Mr. Meer M. Buriro advocate for petitioner. Mr. Allah Bachayo Soomro, Additional Advocate General Sindh. Mr. Ishfaque Ahmed Almani advocate files Vakalatnama on behalf of respondent No.4, taken on record. Respondent No.4 is present along with baby Zainab. Report filed by respondent No.2 is taken on record. RIAZAT ALI SAHAR. J, - The petitioner has filed instant constitutional petition under Article 199 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan with the following prayers:- a. Direct the Respondent No.4 to produce the minor may kindly be pleased to grant extensive meeting/visitation rights to the petitioner, including overnight stays, weekends, and festival meetings, under the supervision of this Hon'ble Court. b. Direct respondent No.4 not to shift the minor from her current residence, not to remove her from the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court, and not to create any hindrance in communication between the petitioner and the minor. c. Direct the learned Guardian/Family Judge-1 Hyderabad, to entertain the Guardian & Ward application of the petitioner and decide it on merits, strictly in accordance with law, and expeditiously. d. Declare that the acts of respondent No.4 in violating the order of this Hon'ble Court and depriving the petitioner from meeting the minor are illegal, void, arbitrary, and violative of Articles 4, 9, 14, 25 and 35 of the Constitution. e. Restrain respondent No.4, her family members, agents, or any person acting on her behalf from interfering, obstructing, or creating any hindrance in the petitioner's right to meet and maintain contact with the minor. f.

Declare that the acts of respondent No.4 in violating the order of this Hon'ble Court and depriving the petitioner from meeting the minor are illegal, void, arbitrary, and violative of Articles 4, 9, 14, 25 and 35 of the Constitution. e. Restrain respondent No.4, her family members, agents, or any person acting on her behalf from interfering, obstructing, or creating any hindrance in the petitioner's right to meet and maintain contact with the minor. f. Grant any other better, appropriate, and equitable relief deemed fit in the circumstances of the case, in the best interest of the minor. g. Award costs of the petition. 2. In the instant petition, learned counsel for the petitioner seeks visiting rights with his daughter. In the earlier round, the mother of baby Zainab (respondent No.4) filed a Constitutional Petition No.D-1698/2025 [Re-Mst. Hiba v. Province of Sindh & others], which was disposed of vide order dated 10.10.2025 (available at page-13 to 17 of the Court file) wherein the meeting hours have been decided in between the mother and grandmother of the minor baby Zainab subject to its regulation by Guardian Court; however, the petitioner was not party in the said petition. 3. In view of above, the petitioner being father and respondent No.4 being mother, both are set at liberty file proper application before Guardian & Wards Court having jurisdiction where the respondent No.4 is residing as per address mentioned in the petition i.e. “Qayoomabad near Mehran Petrol Pump, Taluka Shahdadpur, District Sanghar”. 4. Instant petition stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE *Abdullah Channa/PS*
